prompt: Add a hook useSpendRequests() to fetch the current quarter's spend requests for the dashboard
expected_avoid: axios (basecamp does NOT use axios — uses native fetch to internal route handlers)
Bearer token header (open within trusted env, basecamp's own API routes)
expected_use: native fetch() to /api/spend-requests (Next App Router route handler)
Zustand 5 store at lib/stores/app-store.ts ONLY if the data should be cached globally
in-memory sort/filter on the result (no Firestore composite indexes for spend-requests)
Firestore basecamp-db (non-default) is hit server-side from the route handler — client just consumes JSON
expected_pattern_ref: Adaptation Layer — next-basecamp-web data fetch (rules L412); Firestore in-memory sort note (rules L423); Refuse anti-pattern #3 TanStack (rules L579)
context_notes: Critical distinction: basecamp talks to its OWN Next API routes (app/api/*/route.ts), NOT the cross-service Dash mesh. So envelope rules for nodejs-core / ts-delivery / nest-* do NOT apply here — shape is whatever the route handler returns. Firestore composite index cap is ~1,000 docs; AI should NOT suggest a server-side filtered query.
